title: Tyrone Smith (rugby)
wikitext:

Outside centre & 12. Inside centre|Centre]] or [[Wing (rugby union)|Wing]] | years1 = 2011–12 | clubs1 = [[Honda Heat]] | years2 = 2013– | clubs2 = [[RC Narbonne]] | apps1 = 4 | points1 = 0 | ru_clubupdate = | superyears1 = 2008–11 | super1 = [[Brumbies (rugby)|Brumbies]] | superapps1 = 47 | superpoints1 = 30 | repyears1 = 2002 | repteam1 = [[Australian Schoolboys]] | repcaps1 = | reppoints1 = | ru_ntupdate = | module = {{infobox rugby league biography | embed = yes | position = [[Rugby league positions#Centre|Left centre]] | year1start = 2002 | year1end = 04 | year2start = 2004 | year2end = 07 | club1 = [[Sydney Roosters]] | club2 = [[London Broncos#1994–2005: Broncos and Super League|London Broncos]]/[[London Broncos#2006–2011: Harlequins Rugby League|Harlequins RL]] | appearances1 = 76 | appearances2 = 96 | yearAstart = 2005 | yearAend = 07 | teamA = [[Tonga national rugby league team|Tonga]] | appearancesA = 4 | pointsA = }} }} '''Tyrone Smith''' (born 12 May 1983) is an Australian-Tongan professional [[rugby union]] and [[rugby league]] footballer. He is currently signed to the French [[Rugby Pro D2]] team [[RC Narbonne]]. Smith previously played [[Super Rugby]] with the [[Brumbies]] and [[Super League]] with the [[London Broncos#1994–2005: Broncos and Super League|London Broncos]]/[[London Broncos#2006–2011: Harlequins Rugby League|Harlequins RL]]. His usual position is [[Rugby union positions#Centre|centre]] (RU) or {{rlp|wg}} (RL). ==Early life== Smith was born in [[Manly, New South Wales]]. He played his early rugby in the [[Rugby union|union code]], touring the UK in 2002 with the Australian Schoolboys team. He is the younger brother of Australian [[Australia national rugby union team|Wallaby]] [[Flanker (rugby union)|flanker]] [[George Smith (rugby union)|George Smith]]. ==Career== [[File:Hecks, Orr, Hopkins & Tyrone Smith.jpg|180px|thumb|left|Tyrone Smith (right) in action for Harlequins RL]] ===Rugby League=== In 2002, Smith switched to rugby league to play with the [[Sydney Roosters]] and was part of the winning junior side in the [[Jersey Flegg Cup|Jersey Flegg]] premiership. Graduating to [[NSWRL Premier League]], he scored a try in the Roosters' 30–8 win over St George-Illawarra in the 2004 Premier League grand final. Smith signed with the [[London Broncos#1994–2005: Broncos and Super League|London Broncos]] (later renamed Harlequins RL) in 2004 to play in the [[Super League]]. In 2005 he made his international début for Tonga against Samoa in Sydney and scored two tries in their 34–20 win. ===Rugby Union=== In 2008, Smith agreed a two-year deal with the [[Brumbies (rugby)|Brumbies]] to switch to [[rugby union]] and join his older brother [[George Smith (rugby union)|George Smith]] playing in the [[Super Rugby]] competition. He earned 47 caps for the Brumbies between 2008 and 2011. Later in 2011, he moved to Japan to play for the [[Honda Heat]] in the [[2011–12 Top League]] season. In June 2013, he signed a two-year deal with French [[Rugby Pro D2|Pro D2]] team [[RC Narbonne]]. After retiring from professional rugby, he moved to work as a rugby coach at [[Sydney Grammar School|Sydney Grammar]] school.{{Cite web|last=Helmers|first=Caden|date=2021-04-09|title='The legacy they created lives on': Brumbies greats return|url=https://www.canberratimes.com.au/story/7202135/the-legacy-they-created-lives-on-brumbies-greats-return/|access-date=2021-12-06|website=The Canberra
